title: Introducing synopR
text: Decoding synopR straightforward. Let’s start SYNOP messages retrieved Ogimet Rio Colorado station, Argentina (WMO identifier: 87736). downloading saving data .txt file, can read using readLines(). Take look data. can observe two specific characteristics. First, NIL messages, contain meteorological data. Second, “pure” SYNOP strings; include prefix added Ogimet specifies station ID (87736) along date time observation. However, issue, can use parse_ogimet() function. tool specifically designed strip away headers extract raw SYNOP message processing. decoding, good practice check SYNOP messages non-standard structures. Occasionally, errors occur data groups separated spaces contain 4 digits instead required 5. check_synop() function designed handle cases, ensuring integrity data processing. check_synop() function accepts either character vector specific data frame column containing SYNOP strings. data frame multiple columns —SYNOP column explicitly specified— accepted data frame direct output parse_ogimet(). far, messages correct structure (even NIL ones). Now, let’s see happens don’t. check_synop() returns tibble first column indicates whether SYNOP valid (TRUE) (FALSE), second column describes specific error found. example: first SYNOP correct. second, missing space groups 6 8 Section 1. third, group 2 Section 3 contains 4 digits. fourth message missing “=” terminator (remember SYNOP messages must always start “AAXX” end “=”). fifth simply SYNOP string . Now, ready extract information contained messages. show_synop_data() function real work begins. wmo_identifier argument might seem like extra step, offers significant advantage: allows automatic filtering case data contains messages stations. following example uses vector two messages simplicity, working thousands SYNOP strings multiple stations, built-filtering becomes extremely convenient.
code: # Temporary file synop_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".txt") writeLines(c("87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360=", "87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=", "87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL="), synop_file)  # Read the file data_input <- data.frame(synops = readLines(synop_file))  print(data_input) #>                                                                                                                       synops #> 1                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL= #> 2                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL= #> 3                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL= #> 4 87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360= #> 5                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL= #> 6             87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818= #> 7                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL= library(synopR)  # Note that `parse_ogimet(data_input)` is incorrect data_from_ogimet <- parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)   print(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>    Year Month Day_Ogimet Hour_Ogimet Raw_synop                                   #>   <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>                                       #> 1  2026     2          1           3 AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=                       #> 2  2026     2          1           6 AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=                       #> 3  2026     2          1           9 AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=                       #> 4  2026     2          1          12 AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 3… #> 5  2026     2          1          15 AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=                       #> 6  2026     2          1          18 AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 3… #> 7  2026     2          1          21 AAXX 01214 87736 NIL= check_synop(data_from_ogimet$Raw_synop) #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     ""        #> 3 TRUE     ""        #> 4 TRUE     ""        #> 5 TRUE     ""        #> 6 TRUE     ""        #> 7 TRUE     ""  check_synop(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     ""        #> 3 TRUE     ""        #> 4 TRUE     ""        #> 5 TRUE     ""        #> 6 TRUE     ""        #> 7 TRUE     "" my_df <- data.frame(syn = c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                             "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818="),                     second_column = c(5,7))  check_synop(my_df) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                                 #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                     #> 1 FALSE    Missing AAXX | Missing '=' terminator | Invalid groups: 5 #> 2 FALSE    Missing AAXX | Missing '=' terminator | Invalid groups: 7  check_synop(my_df$syn) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     "" check_synop(c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 6000182100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 2021 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818",               "Not a synop message=")) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                               #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                   #> 1 TRUE     ""                                                      #> 2 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 6000182100"                            #> 3 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 2021"                                  #> 4 FALSE    "Missing '=' terminator"                                #> 5 FALSE    "Missing AAXX | Invalid groups: Not, a, synop, message" my_data <- show_synop_data(data_from_ogimet, wmo_identifier = '87736')  knitr::kable(t(my_data)) # Messages from 87736 and 87016 mixed_synop <- c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                  "AAXX 04033 87016 41460 83208 10200 20194 39712 40114 50003 70292 888// 333 56699 82810 88615="                  )  colorado_data <- show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = '87736') #> Warning in show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = "87736"): 1 message(s) #> do not contain the identifier '87736' and will be discarded. print(colorado_data) #> # A tibble: 1 × 29 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      1    18                 4         65                 2 #> # ℹ 23 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, #> #   Sea_level_pressure <dbl>, Present_weather <dbl>, Past_weather1 <dbl>, #> #   Past_weather2 <dbl>, Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Ground_state <dbl>, Ground_temperature <dbl>, Snow_ground_state <dbl>, …

title: Introducing synopR
text: show_synop_data() requires character vector data frame column element SYNOP string. Group 555 (reserved national distribution) currently ignored, content varies country. However, future versions synopR may include functions extract data section based user requirements.
code: data_input_vector <- c("AAXX 04003 87736 32965 00000 10204 20106 39982 40074 5//// 333 10266 20158 555 64169 65090 =",                        "AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 81270 =")  my_data <- show_synop_data(data_input_vector, wmo_identifier = '87736')  print(my_data) #> # A tibble: 3 × 29 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      4     0                 9         65                 0 #> 2 87736      1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736      3    18                 9         65                 1 #> # ℹ 23 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, #> #   Sea_level_pressure <dbl>, Present_weather <dbl>, Past_weather1 <dbl>, #> #   Past_weather2 <dbl>, Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Ground_state <dbl>, Ground_temperature <dbl>, Snow_ground_state <dbl>, …

title: Introducing synopR
text: synopR don’t accept sections 222 y 444. Please visit official GitHub repository information regarding limitations assumptions.

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Decodificar mensajes SYNOP con synopR es muy sencillo. Supongamos que necesitamos información meteorológica de la estación Rio Colorado (identificador WMO: 87736) para lo cual recurrimos Ogimet. Por simplicidad, sólo descargamos los SYNOP del 1 de febrero del 2026, utilizando este link. Guardamos la información en un .txt y leemos este archivo con readLines(). Tenemos un dataframe con una sola columna y tantas filas como mensajes. Sin embargo, podemos ver dos particularidades. La primera, es que hay mensajes NIL, sin contenido. La segunda, es que son SYNOP puros, sino que previamente tienen un agregado que hace Ogimet, donde se indica la estación (87736) y la fecha y hora de la observación. Pero hay problema, porque podemos usar la función parse_ogimet(), especialmente diseñada para separar este agregado del mensaje SYNOP. Antes de decodificar los mensajes, quizás sea conveniente chequear la estructura de los SYNOP. Por ejemplo, puede pasar que, por error, algunos de los grupos estén separados por un espacio, o que contengan 4 cifras en lugar de 5. La función check_synop() se encargará de esto. check_synop() toma como argumento un character vector o la columna de un dataframe donde están los SYNOP. Un dataframe de varias columnas sin indicación de cual es la columna de los SYNOP es aceptado solo y solo si ese dataframe es el resultado de parse_ogimet(). Hasta ahora, nuestros mensajes tienen una estructura correcta (aunque algunos sean NIL). Veamos que pasa cuando la tienen. check_synop() devuelve un tibble que en su primera columna informa la validez (TRUE) o (FALSE) de cada synop, y en su segunda columna, el error encontrado. El primer SYNOP es correcto. En el segundo hay un espacio entre los grupos 6 y 8 de la sección 1. En el tercero, el grupo 2 de la sección 3 tiene sólo 4 cifras. En el cuarto, termina con un “=” (recordar que siempre deben comenzar con “AAXX” y terminar con un “=”). El quinto, es cualquier cosa menos un SYNOP. Ya estamos listos para extraer la información contenida en los mensajes. La función show_synop_data() es la que se pondrá manos la obra. El argumento wmo_identifier puede parecer molesto, pero tiene una ventaja. Permite filtrar en caso de que hayan mensajes de otras estaciones. Si bien en el siguiente ejemplo, por cuestiones de simplicidad, tenemos un vector con solamente 2 mensajes, si estás trabajando con miles y miles de SYNOP de varias estaciones, que la función permita aplicar este tipo de filtro podría ser muy conveniente.
code: # Tenemos el archivo con los datos archivos_synop <- tempfile(fileext = ".txt") writeLines(c("87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360=", "87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=", "87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=", "87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL="), archivos_synop)  # Lectura del archivo data_input <- data.frame(synops = readLines(archivos_synop)) # Dataframe con una sola columna  print(data_input) #>                                                                                                                       synops #> 1                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL= #> 2                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL= #> 3                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL= #> 4 87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360= #> 5                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL= #> 6             87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818= #> 7                                                                               87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL= library(synopR)  # Notar que `parse_ogimet()` toma como argumento un character vector, es incorrecto hacer `show_synop_data(data_input)` data_from_ogimet <- parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)   print(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>    Year Month Day_Ogimet Hour_Ogimet Raw_synop                                   #>   <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>                                       #> 1  2026     2          1           3 AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=                       #> 2  2026     2          1           6 AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=                       #> 3  2026     2          1           9 AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=                       #> 4  2026     2          1          12 AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 3… #> 5  2026     2          1          15 AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=                       #> 6  2026     2          1          18 AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 3… #> 7  2026     2          1          21 AAXX 01214 87736 NIL= check_synop(data_from_ogimet$Raw_synop) #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     ""        #> 3 TRUE     ""        #> 4 TRUE     ""        #> 5 TRUE     ""        #> 6 TRUE     ""        #> 7 TRUE     ""  check_synop(data_from_ogimet) #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     ""        #> 3 TRUE     ""        #> 4 TRUE     ""        #> 5 TRUE     ""        #> 6 TRUE     ""        #> 7 TRUE     "" my_df <- data.frame(syn = c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                             "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818="),                     second_column = c(5,7))  check_synop(my_df) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                                 #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                     #> 1 FALSE    Missing AAXX | Missing '=' terminator | Invalid groups: 5 #> 2 FALSE    Missing AAXX | Missing '=' terminator | Invalid groups: 7  check_synop(my_df$syn) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log #>   <lgl>    <chr>     #> 1 TRUE     ""        #> 2 TRUE     "" check_synop(c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 6000182100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 2021 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",               "AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818",               "Not a synop message=")) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   is_valid error_log                                               #>   <lgl>    <chr>                                                   #> 1 TRUE     ""                                                      #> 2 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 6000182100"                            #> 3 FALSE    "Invalid groups: 2021"                                  #> 4 FALSE    "Missing '=' terminator"                                #> 5 FALSE    "Missing AAXX | Invalid groups: Not, a, synop, message" my_data <- show_synop_data(data_from_ogimet, wmo_identifier = '87736')  knitr::kable(t(my_data)) # Mensaje de la estación 87736 y de la estación 87016. mixed_synop <- c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                  "AAXX 04033 87016 41460 83208 10200 20194 39712 40114 50003 70292 888// 333 56699 82810 88615="                  )  colorado_data <- show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = '87736') #> Warning in show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = "87736"): 1 message(s) #> do not contain the identifier '87736' and will be discarded. print(colorado_data) #> # A tibble: 1 × 29 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      1    18                 4         65                 2 #> # ℹ 23 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, #> #   Sea_level_pressure <dbl>, Present_weather <dbl>, Past_weather1 <dbl>, #> #   Past_weather2 <dbl>, Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Ground_state <dbl>, Ground_temperature <dbl>, Snow_ground_state <dbl>, …

title: Introducción a synopR
text: Todo lo que necesita show_synop_data() es un character vector o la columna de un dataframe, donde cada elemento es un SYNOP. El grupo 555, de difusión nacional, es ignorado, puesto que depende de cada país. Sin embargo, es posible que en futuras versiones de synopR se añadan funciones que permitan extraer datos de esta sección según las necesidades del usuario.
code: data_input_vector <- c("AAXX 04003 87736 32965 00000 10204 20106 39982 40074 5//// 333 10266 20158 555 64169 65090 =",                        "AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 81270 =")  my_data <- show_synop_data(data_input_vector, wmo_identifier = '87736')  print(my_data) #> # A tibble: 3 × 29 #>   wmo_id   Day  Hour Cloud_base_height Visibility Total_cloud_cover #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>      <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 87736      4     0                 9         65                 0 #> 2 87736      1     9                NA         NA                NA #> 3 87736      3    18                 9         65                 1 #> # ℹ 23 more variables: Wind_direction <dbl>, Wind_speed <dbl>, #> #   Air_temperature <dbl>, Dew_point <dbl>, Station_pressure <dbl>, #> #   Sea_level_pressure <dbl>, Present_weather <dbl>, Past_weather1 <dbl>, #> #   Past_weather2 <dbl>, Precipitation_S1 <dbl>, Precip_period_S1 <dbl>, #> #   Cloud_amount_Nh <dbl>, Low_clouds_CL <dbl>, Medium_clouds_CM <dbl>, #> #   High_clouds_CH <dbl>, Max_temperature <dbl>, Min_temperature <dbl>, #> #   Ground_state <dbl>, Ground_temperature <dbl>, Snow_ground_state <dbl>, …

title: Introducción a synopR
text: synopR trabaja con las secciones 222 y 444. Otras limitaciones y suposiciones pueden ser encontradas en el repositorio oficial en Github.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ezequiel Elias. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Elias E (2026). synopR: Tidy Decoding SYNOP Meteorological Messages. R package version 0.1.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {synopR: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages},   author = {Ezequiel Elias},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: goal synopR provide simple robust tool decoding SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) messages, specifically optimized data retrieved Ogimet standard WMO formats. focuses extracting Sections 0, 1 3 tidy, analysis-ready format.

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: can install development version synopR GitHub :

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: package extracts following meteorological parameters:

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: Clouds: Lower cloud base height (h), Total coverage (N), Low medium cloud amount (Nh), types (Cl, Cm, Ch). Visibility: Horizontal visibility (VV). Wind: Direction (dd) speed (ff) knots. Temperature: Air temperature (TTT) Dew point (TdTdTd) °C. Pressure: MSL pressure (PPPP) Surface pressure (PPPP1) hPa. Precipitation: Amount (RRR1) time window (tR1) hours. Weather: Present (ww) past weather (W1, W2).

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: Extremes: Maximum (Tx) Minimum (Tn) temperature °C. Ground: State ground (E, E'), snow depth (sss) ground minimum temperature (TgTg). Section 3 Precipitation: Amount (RRR3) time window (tR3). Note: Parameters like h, VV, N, Nh, ww, W1, W2, E, E', Cl, Cm, Ch extracted original coded format WMO tables. N Nh, values 0-8 directly represent coverage oktas.

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: package includes parser specifically designed comma-separated format used Ogimet:
code: library(synopR)  # Example Ogimet string # Downloaded from: https://www.ogimet.com/cgi-bin/getsynop?block=87736&begin=202601011800&end=202601011800 raw_data <- "87736,2026,01,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 11463 41813 10330 20148 39982 40072 5//// 60001 70700 83105 333 56600 83818="  # Parse and decode decoded <- parse_ogimet(raw_data) |>   show_synop_data(wmo_identifier = "87736")  print(decoded)

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: ensure accurate decoding, package assumes: Sections: package support sections 222 (Maritime data) 444 (Cloud data). Format: package automatically discards national data sections (Section 555) filters NIL reports. Timing: Observations assumed occur time indicated Section 0 (Group 9 time-shifts currently ignored). Wind: Speed (ff) reported knots < 100 kt. Humidity: Group 2 (Section 1) contains dew point, relative humidity. Pressure: Group 4 (Section 1) reports MSL pressure, geopotential height. Precipitation (Trace): Values coded 990 converted 0.01 (mm) allow numerical analysis representing non-zero values. Regionality: Section 3, Group 3 interpreted ground minimum temperature.

title: Tidy Decoding of SYNOP Meteorological Messages
text: can check structural integrity SYNOP messages decoding:

what: Check SYNOP messages for structural integrity — check_synop
title: Check SYNOP messages for structural integrity — check_synop
text: Validates SYNOP strings meet basic structural requirements, considering section indicators (222, 333, 444, 555) 5-digit data groups.

title: Check SYNOP messages for structural integrity — check_synop
Check SYNOP messages for structural integrity — check_synop
text: data character vector data frame containing SYNOP strings.

title: Check SYNOP messages for structural integrity — check_synop
text: tibble validation results message.

what: Parse Ogimet CSV strings into a data frame — parse_ogimet
title: Parse Ogimet CSV strings into a data frame — parse_ogimet
text: Parse Ogimet CSV strings data frame

title: Parse Ogimet CSV strings into a data frame — parse_ogimet
Parse Ogimet CSV strings into a data frame — parse_ogimet
text: ogimet_data character vector Ogimet strings.

title: Parse Ogimet CSV strings into a data frame — parse_ogimet
text: tibble Year, Month, Day, Hour, Raw_Synop.

what: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: function decodes vector data frame column raw SYNOP strings belonging WMO station. efficiently processes multiple observations , returning tidy data frame.

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: data character vector, data frame tibble one column (V1) containing raw SYNOP strings. wmo_identifier 5-digit character string (e.g., "87736") representing station WMO ID.

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: tidy tibble row represents one observation time column decoded meteorological variable.

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
text: function vectorized purrr::map, meaning can handle number SYNOP messages input data frame, provided belong station specified wmo_identifier. automatically handles Section 0 (Time), Section 1 (Global), Section 3 (Regional).

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages from a single station — show_synop_data
